content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Verified both findings against service.rs and bundle.rs. Amendment fr-7-95596c0d is a real duplication bug: explicit_id beads are never probed by their authored ID, and the title-based index only sees beads already carrying milestone:<id>, so referencing a pre-existing bead creates a duplicate. Amendment fr-7-8f68ada9 is a real idempotency bug: shared_materialized_labels requires every non-identity input label to exist on the summary, so adding any new label to a proposal falls through to the 'incompatible active matches' error even though the identity label (proposal:/workstream:) still uniquely identifies the same bead.

## Votes

- `fr-7-95596c0d`: Accept
  Confirmed: in service.rs:1543-1561 the existing-bead index is built only from beads already carrying milestone_scope_label, and in service.rs:1646-1706 proposal.bead_id is consumed only to derive proposal_label / report keys — never to probe the authored bead via br show. Combined with the bundle.rs:615-620 comment acknowledging that explicit beads may pre-exist under their short ID, this means an explicit_id reference to an unlabeled bead will silently create a duplicate. Dependencies and planning comments then attach to the wrong bead. High severity is appropriate because it corrupts the graph and the remediation (resolve explicit authored ID first) is straightforward.
- `fr-7-8f68ada9`: Accept
  Confirmed: summary_matches_materialized_bead_except_role at service.rs:2012-2020 applies an 'input labels ⊆ summary labels' check over shared_materialized_labels, which excludes only milestone-root and identity labels. Any newly added proposal label therefore drops the otherwise-matching bead out of eligible_indices, and the fallback at service.rs:2127-2143 converts the remaining active identity-labeled bead into the 'incompatible active matches' hard error. Because identity labels (proposal:<id> / workstream:<id>) already guarantee uniqueness, this over-constraint breaks replay/idempotency for any routine plan edit. Medium severity is appropriate and the proposed fix (use identity label as primary reuse key, reconcile metadata drift) is the right direction.
